methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Focused Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Thomas Forner and Markus Roth that has raised $80M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy firms (3 public, 8 private), including peers such as Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The primary applicable method is stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($80M+) as a proxy for Series A enterprise value of approximately $1.0B, a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier to reflect progression toward Series B yields an implied valuation of $2.0B. The peer-median method could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow nuclear energy segment; expanding the peer set would risk introducing valuation noise from structurally dissimilar companies.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $1.7B (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $2.0B. This range reflects dependence on a single quantitative method and the inherent sensitivity to the $1.0B base-value assumption. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-transition multiple for early-stage nuclear ventures, the representativeness of total raised as a valuation proxy, and the sparse density of comparable Series A financings in the sector. Investors should treat this range as preliminary pending additional market data or operational milestones.
